answer:
The ghazal is found in Allama Iqbal's *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, chapter 6 ("Zamana dekhe ga jab mere dil se mahshar uthe ga guftugu ka").[^1]

The wave and pearl debate occurs in the following couplet:

> The river's wave began to say, "My glory comes from journeying,"  
> The pearl replied, "To stay inside is the source of my honor's sheen."[^2]

Later, the rose and blood imagery appears here:

> Such Perfect Oneness is revealed, if with a needle you but prod,  
> From the rose's vein, I know for sure, would fall a drop of human blood.[^3]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, [chapter 6](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/06-zamana-dekhe-ga-jab-mere-dil-se-mahshar-uthe-ga-gu/translation/).

[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/06-zamana-dekhe-ga-jab-mere-dil-se-mahshar-uthe-ga-gu/translation/#p-26a389).

[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/06-zamana-dekhe-ga-jab-mere-dil-se-mahshar-uthe-ga-gu/translation/#p-30ffa7).
